verses.Job 7:3: So am I made to possess months of vanity, and wearisome nights are appointed to me.

verses.Job 8:10: Shall not they teach thee, and tell thee, and utter words out of their heart?

verses.Job 10:3: Is it good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress, that thou shouldest despise the work of thine hands, and shine upon the counsel of the wicked?
verses.Job 10:4: Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?

verses.Job 10:7: Thou knowest that I am not wicked; and there is none that can deliver out of thine hand.
verses.Job 10:14: If I sin, then thou markest me, and thou wilt not acquit me from mine iniquity.
verses.Job 10:15: If I be wicked, woe unto me; and if I be righteous, yet will I not lift up my head. I am full of confusion; therefore see thou mine affliction;
verses.Job 10:18: Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? Oh that I had given up the ghost, and no eye had seen me!
verses.Job 10:22: A land of darkness, as darkness itself; and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness.
verses.Job 11:6: And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that they are double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine …

verses.Job 11:15: For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea, thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not fear:
verses.Job 11:17: And thine age shall be clearer than the noonday: thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning.
verses.Job 11:18: And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.
verses.Job 11:19: Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee.
verses.Job 11:20: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and they shall not escape, and their hope shall be as the giving up of the ghost.
verses.Job 12:8: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee: and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.
verses.Job 12:9: Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the LORD hath wrought this?
verses.Job 12:10: In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind.
verses.Job 13:2: What ye know, the same do I know also: I am not inferior unto you.
verses.Job 13:3: Surely I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to reason with God.
verses.Job 13:4: But ye are forgers of lies, ye are all physicians of no value.
verses.Job 13:7: Will ye speak wickedly for God? and talk deceitfully for him?
verses.Job 13:20: Only do not two things unto me: then will I not hide myself from thee.
verses.Job 13:22: Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.
verses.Job 13:24: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?
verses.Job 13:25: Wilt thou break a leaf driven to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?
verses.Job 13:26: For thou writest bitter things against me, and makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth.
verses.Job 14:7: For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.

verses.Job 14:20: Thou prevailest for ever against him, and he passeth: thou changest his countenance, and sendest him away.

verses.Job 14:22: But his flesh upon him shall have pain, and his soul within him shall mourn.
verses.Job 15:5: For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty.

verses.Job 15:31: Let not him that is deceived trust in vanity: for vanity shall be his recompence.
verses.Job 16:2: I have heard many such things: miserable comforters are ye all.

verses.Job 16:16: My face is foul with weeping, and on my eyelids is the shadow of death;

verses.Job 16:22: When a few years are come, then I shall go the way whence I shall not return.

verses.Job 18:11: Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall drive him to his feet.

verses.Job 18:16: His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.

verses.Job 18:21: Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked, and this is the place of him that knoweth not God.

verses.Job 21:4: As for me, is my complaint to man? and if it were so, why should not my spirit be troubled?

verses.Job 22:5: Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities infinite?

verses.Job 30:2: Yea, whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom old age was perished?
verses.Job 30:15: Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud.

verses.Job 31:35: Oh that one would hear me! behold, my desire is, that the Almighty would answer me, and that mine adversary had written a book.

verses.Job 32:7: I said, Days should speak, and multitude of years should teach wisdom.

verses.Job 35:16: Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vain; he multiplieth words without knowledge.

verses.Job 42:1: Then Job answered the LORD, and said,

verses.Psalms 2:4: He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh: the LORD shall have them in derision.
verses.Psalms 2:7: I will declare the decree: the LORD hath said unto me, Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee.
verses.Psalms 2:10: Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be instructed, ye judges of the earth.
verses.Psalms 3:1: Lord, how are they increased that trouble me! many are they that rise up against me.
verses.Psalms 3:2: Many there be which say of my soul, There is no help for him in God. Selah.
verses.Psalms 3:3: But thou, O LORD, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.
verses.Psalms 4:1: Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast enlarged me when I was in distress; have mercy upon me, and hear my prayer.
verses.Psalms 4:3: But know that the LORD hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the LORD will hear when I call unto him.
verses.Psalms 4:5: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD.
verses.Psalms 4:6: There be many that say, Who will shew us any good? LORD, lift thou up the light of thy countenance upon us.

verses.Psalms 6:10: Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore vexed: let them return and be ashamed suddenly.
verses.Psalms 7:3: O LORD my God, If I have done this; if there be iniquity in my hands;

verses.Psalms 7:15: He made a pit, and digged it, and is fallen into the ditch which he made.

verses.Psalms 8:7: All sheep and oxen, yea, and the beasts of the field;

verses.Psalms 9:8: And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.

verses.Psalms 10:1: Why standest thou afar off, O LORD? why hidest thou thyself in times of trouble?
verses.Psalms 10:6: He hath said in his heart, I shall not be moved: for I shall never be in adversity.
verses.Psalms 10:7: His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and fraud: under his tongue is mischief and vanity.
verses.Psalms 10:8: He sitteth in the lurking places of the villages: in the secret places doth he murder the innocent: his eyes are privily set against the poor.
verses.Psalms 10:11: He hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten: he hideth his face; he will never see it.
verses.Psalms 10:16: The LORD is King for ever and ever: the heathen are perished out of his land.
verses.Psalms 10:18: To judge the fatherless and the oppressed, that the man of the earth may no more oppress.
verses.Psalms 11:3: If the foundations be destroyed, what can the righteous do?
verses.Psalms 11:4: The LORD is in his holy temple, the LORD’s throne is in heaven: his eyes behold, his eyelids try, the children of men.
verses.Psalms 12:6: The words of the LORD are pure words: as silver tried in a furnace of earth, purified seven times.
verses.Psalms 12:7: Thou shalt keep them, O LORD, thou shalt preserve them from this generation for ever.
verses.Psalms 12:8: The wicked walk on every side, when the vilest men are exalted.
verses.Psalms 13:1: How long wilt thou forget me, O LORD? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
verses.Psalms 13:5: But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.
verses.Psalms 15:1: Lord, who shall abide in thy tabernacle? who shall dwell in thy holy hill?
verses.Psalms 15:3: He that backbiteth not with his tongue, nor doeth evil to his neighbour, nor taketh up a reproach against his neighbour.
verses.Psalms 15:5: He that putteth not out his money to usury, nor taketh reward against the innocent. He that doeth these things shall never be moved.
verses.Psalms 16:8: I have set the LORD always before me: because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.
verses.Psalms 16:11: Thou wilt shew me the path of life: in thy presence is fulness of joy; at thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore.
verses.Psalms 17:1: Hear the right, O LORD, attend unto my cry, give ear unto my prayer, that goeth not out of feigned lips.
verses.Psalms 17:5: Hold up my goings in thy paths, that my footsteps slip not.
verses.Psalms 17:6: I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
verses.Psalms 17:8: Keep me as the apple of the eye, hide me under the shadow of thy wings,
verses.Psalms 17:12: Like as a lion that is greedy of his prey, and as it were a young lion lurking in secret places.
verses.Psalms 17:13: Arise, O LORD, disappoint him, cast him down: deliver my soul from the wicked, which is thy sword:
verses.Psalms 18:6: In my distress I called upon the LORD, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.
verses.Psalms 18:25: With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful; with an upright man thou wilt shew thyself upright;
verses.Psalms 18:26: With the pure thou wilt shew thyself pure; and with the froward thou wilt shew thyself froward.
verses.Psalms 18:29: For by thee I have run through a troop; and by my God have I leaped over a wall.
verses.Psalms 18:31: For who is God save the LORD? or who is a rock save our God?
verses.Psalms 18:37: I have pursued mine enemies, and overtaken them: neither did I turn again till they were consumed.
verses.Psalms 18:45: The strangers shall fade away, and be afraid out of their close places.
verses.Psalms 18:49: Therefore will I give thanks unto thee, O LORD, among the heathen, and sing praises unto thy name.
verses.Psalms 19:8: The statutes of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart: the commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes.
verses.Psalms 20:9: Save, LORD: let the king hear us when we call.
verses.Psalms 21:5: His glory is great in thy salvation: honour and majesty hast thou laid upon him.
verses.Psalms 21:7: For the king trusteth in the LORD, and through the mercy of the most High he shall not be moved.
verses.Psalms 21:8: Thine hand shall find out all thine enemies: thy right hand shall find out those that hate thee.
verses.Psalms 21:9: Thou shalt make them as a fiery oven in the time of thine anger: the LORD shall swallow them up in his wrath, and the fire shall devour them.
verses.Psalms 22:2: O my God, I cry in the day time, but thou hearest not; and in the night season, and am not silent.
verses.Psalms 22:10: I was cast upon thee from the womb: thou art my God from my mother’s belly.
verses.Psalms 22:11: Be not far from me; for trouble is near; for there is none to help.
verses.Psalms 22:15: My strength is dried up like a potsherd; and my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; and thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
verses.Psalms 22:18: They part my garments among them, and cast lots upon my vesture.
verses.Psalms 22:24: For he hath not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; neither hath he hid his face from him; but when he cried unto him, he heard.
verses.Psalms 22:26: The meek shall eat and be satisfied: they shall praise the LORD that seek him: your heart shall live for ever.
verses.Psalms 22:29: All they that be fat upon earth shall eat and worship: all they that go down to the dust shall bow before him: and none can keep alive his own soul.
verses.Psalms 23:1: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
verses.Psalms 23:3: He restoreth my soul: he leadeth me in the paths of righteousness for his name’s sake.
verses.Psalms 23:4: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil: for thou art with me; thy rod and thy staff they comfort me.
verses.Psalms 23:6: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me all the days of my life: and I will dwell in the house of the LORD for ever.
verses.Psalms 24:1: The earth is the LORD’s, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein.
verses.Psalms 24:3: Who shall ascend into the hill of the LORD? or who shall stand in his holy place?
verses.Psalms 24:7: Lift up your heads, O ye gates; and be ye lift up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.
verses.Psalms 24:9: Lift up your heads, O ye gates; even lift them up, ye everlasting doors; and the King of glory shall come in.
verses.Psalms 24:10: Who is this King of glory? The LORD of hosts, he is the King of glory. Selah.
verses.Psalms 25:1: Unto thee, O LORD, do I lift up my soul.
verses.Psalms 25:9: The meek will he guide in judgment: and the meek will he teach his way.
verses.Psalms 25:13: His soul shall dwell at ease; and his seed shall inherit the earth.
verses.Psalms 25:16: Turn thee unto me, and have mercy upon me; for I am desolate and afflicted.
verses.Psalms 25:20: O keep my soul, and deliver me: let me not be ashamed; for I put my trust in thee.
verses.Psalms 26:7: That I may publish with the voice of thanksgiving, and tell of all thy wondrous works.
verses.Psalms 26:8: LORD, I have loved the habitation of thy house, and the place where thine honour dwelleth.
verses.Psalms 27:4: One thing have I desired of the LORD, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to behold the beau…
verses.Psalms 27:6: And now shall mine head be lifted up above mine enemies round about me: therefore will I offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of joy; I will sing, ye…
verses.Psalms 27:8: When thou saidst, Seek ye my face; my heart said unto thee, Thy face, LORD, will I seek.
verses.Psalms 27:10: When my father and my mother forsake me, then the LORD will take me up.
verses.Psalms 27:14: Wait on the LORD: be of good courage, and he shall strengthen thine heart: wait, I say, on the LORD.
verses.Psalms 28:1: Unto thee will I cry, O LORD my rock; be not silent to me: lest, if thou be silent to me, I become like them that go down into the pit.
verses.Psalms 28:4: Give them according to their deeds, and according to the wickedness of their endeavours: give them after the work of their hands; render to them th…
verses.Psalms 28:6: Blessed be the LORD, because he hath heard the voice of my supplications.
verses.Psalms 29:4: The voice of the LORD is powerful; the voice of the LORD is full of majesty.
verses.Psalms 29:5: The voice of the LORD breaketh the cedars; yea, the LORD breaketh the cedars of Lebanon.
verses.Psalms 29:7: The voice of the LORD divideth the flames of fire.
verses.Psalms 29:8: The voice of the LORD shaketh the wilderness; the LORD shaketh the wilderness of Kadesh.
verses.Psalms 29:10: The LORD sitteth upon the flood; yea, the LORD sitteth King for ever.
verses.Psalms 29:11: The LORD will give strength unto his people; the LORD will bless his people with peace.
verses.Psalms 30:4: Sing unto the LORD, O ye saints of his, and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.
verses.Psalms 30:6: And in my prosperity I said, I shall never be moved.
verses.Psalms 30:7: LORD, by thy favour thou hast made my mountain to stand strong: thou didst hide thy face, and I was troubled.
verses.Psalms 30:9: What profit is there in my blood, when I go down to the pit? Shall the dust praise thee? shall it declare thy truth?
verses.Psalms 30:12: To the end that my glory may sing praise to thee, and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks unto thee for ever.
verses.Psalms 31:1: In thee, O LORD, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.
verses.Psalms 31:5: Into thine hand I commit my spirit: thou hast redeemed me, O LORD God of truth.
verses.Psalms 31:7: I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;
verses.Psalms 31:13: For I have heard the slander of many: fear was on every side: while they took counsel together against me, they devised to take away my life.
verses.Psalms 31:14: But I trusted in thee, O LORD: I said, Thou art my God.
verses.Psalms 31:16: Make thy face to shine upon thy servant: save me for thy mercies’ sake.
verses.Psalms 31:19: Oh how great is thy goodness, which thou hast laid up for them that fear thee; which thou hast wrought for them that trust in thee before the sons …
verses.Psalms 31:21: Blessed be the LORD: for he hath shewed me his marvellous kindness in a strong city.
verses.Psalms 31:24: Be of good courage, and he shall strengthen your heart, all ye that hope in the LORD.
verses.Psalms 32:2: Blessed is the man unto whom the LORD imputeth not iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no guile.
verses.Psalms 32:3: When I kept silence, my bones waxed old through my roaring all the day long.
verses.Psalms 33:5: He loveth righteousness and judgment: the earth is full of the goodness of the LORD.
verses.Psalms 33:7: He gathereth the waters of the sea together as an heap: he layeth up the depth in storehouses.
verses.Psalms 33:8: Let all the earth fear the LORD: let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of him.
verses.Psalms 33:9: For he spake, and it was done; he commanded, and it stood fast.
verses.Psalms 33:18: Behold, the eye of the LORD is upon them that fear him, upon them that hope in his mercy;
verses.Psalms 33:19: To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
verses.Psalms 33:21: For our heart shall rejoice in him, because we have trusted in his holy name.
verses.Psalms 33:22: Let thy mercy, O LORD, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
verses.Psalms 34:10: The young lions do lack, and suffer hunger: but they that seek the LORD shall not want any good thing.
verses.Psalms 34:13: Keep thy tongue from evil, and thy lips from speaking guile.
verses.Psalms 34:16: The face of the LORD is against them that do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.
verses.Psalms 34:19: Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the LORD delivereth him out of them all.
verses.Psalms 34:21: Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
verses.Psalms 34:22: The LORD redeemeth the soul of his servants: and none of them that trust in him shall be desolate.
verses.Psalms 35:3: Draw out also the spear, and stop the way against them that persecute me: say unto my soul, I am thy salvation.
verses.Psalms 35:7: For without cause have they hid for me their net in a pit, which without cause they have digged for my soul.
verses.Psalms 35:13: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth: I humbled my soul with fasting; and my prayer returned into mine own bosom.
verses.Psalms 35:17: Lord, how long wilt thou look on? rescue my soul from their destructions, my darling from the lions.
verses.Psalms 35:26: Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselv…
verses.Psalms 36:9: For with thee is the fountain of life: in thy light shall we see light.
verses.Psalms 36:11: Let not the foot of pride come against me, and let not the hand of the wicked remove me.
verses.Psalms 37:2: For they shall soon be cut down like the grass, and wither as the green herb.
verses.Psalms 37:6: And he shall bring forth thy righteousness as the light, and thy judgment as the noonday.
verses.Psalms 37:9: For evildoers shall be cut off: but those that wait upon the LORD, they shall inherit the earth.
verses.Psalms 37:10: For yet a little while, and the wicked shall not be: yea, thou shalt diligently consider his place, and it shall not be.
verses.Psalms 37:11: But the meek shall inherit the earth; and shall delight themselves in the abundance of peace.
verses.Psalms 37:13: The LORD shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.
verses.Psalms 37:15: Their sword shall enter into their own heart, and their bows shall be broken.
verses.Psalms 37:22: For such as be blessed of him shall inherit the earth; and they that be cursed of him shall be cut off.
verses.Psalms 37:23: The steps of a good man are ordered by the LORD: and he delighteth in his way.
verses.Psalms 37:25: I have been young, and now am old; yet have I not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his seed begging bread.
verses.Psalms 37:26: He is ever merciful, and lendeth; and his seed is blessed.
verses.Psalms 37:29: The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein for ever.
verses.Psalms 37:33: The LORD will not leave him in his hand, nor condemn him when he is judged.
verses.Psalms 37:36: Yet he passed away, and, lo, he was not: yea, I sought him, but he could not be found.
verses.Psalms 37:38: But the transgressors shall be destroyed together: the end of the wicked shall be cut off.
verses.Psalms 38:13: But I, as a deaf man, heard not; and I was as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.
verses.Psalms 38:15: For in thee, O LORD, do I hope: thou wilt hear, O Lord my God.
verses.Psalms 38:18: For I will declare mine iniquity; I will be sorry for my sin.
verses.Psalms 38:22: Make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.
verses.Psalms 39:9: I was dumb, I opened not my mouth; because thou didst it.
verses.Psalms 39:12: Hear my prayer, O LORD, and give ear unto my cry; hold not thy peace at my tears: for I am a stranger with thee, and a sojourner, as all my fathers…
verses.Psalms 40:1: I waited patiently for the LORD; and he inclined unto me, and heard my cry.
verses.Psalms 40:2: He brought me up also out of an horrible pit, out of the miry clay, and set my feet upon a rock, and established my goings.
verses.Psalms 40:3: And he hath put a new song in my mouth, even praise unto our God: many shall see it, and fear, and shall trust in the LORD.
verses.Psalms 40:4: Blessed is that man that maketh the LORD his trust, and respecteth not the proud, nor such as turn aside to lies.
verses.Psalms 40:6: Sacrifice and offering thou didst not desire; mine ears hast thou opened: burnt offering and sin offering hast thou not required.
verses.Psalms 40:9: I have preached righteousness in the great congregation: lo, I have not refrained my lips, O LORD, thou knowest.
verses.Psalms 40:13: Be pleased, O LORD, to deliver me: O LORD, make haste to help me.
verses.Psalms 40:15: Let them be desolate for a reward of their shame that say unto me, Aha, aha.
verses.Psalms 40:16: Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: let such as love thy salvation say continually, The LORD be magnified.
verses.Psalms 40:17: But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.
verses.Psalms 41:4: I said, LORD, be merciful unto me: heal my soul; for I have sinned against thee.
verses.Psalms 41:5: Mine enemies speak evil of me, When shall he die, and his name perish?
verses.Psalms 41:6: And if he come to see me, he speaketh vanity: his heart gathereth iniquity to itself; when he goeth abroad, he telleth it.
verses.Psalms 41:12: And as for me, thou upholdest me in mine integrity, and settest me before thy face for ever.
verses.Psalms 42:2: My soul thirsteth for God, for the living God: when shall I come and appear before God?

verses.Psalms 43:3: O send out thy light and thy truth: let them lead me; let them bring me unto thy holy hill, and to thy tabernacles.
verses.Psalms 43:5: Why art thou cast down, O my soul? and why art thou disquieted within me? hope in God: for I shall yet praise him, who is the health of my countena…
verses.Psalms 44:8: In God we boast all the day long, and praise thy name for ever. Selah.
verses.Psalms 44:12: Thou sellest thy people for nought, and dost not increase thy wealth by their price.
verses.Psalms 44:21: Shall not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.
verses.Psalms 44:24: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and forgettest our affliction and our oppression?
verses.Psalms 45:3: Gird thy sword upon thy thigh, O most mighty, with thy glory and thy majesty.
verses.Psalms 45:9: Kings’ daughters were among thy honourable women: upon thy right hand did stand the queen in gold of Ophir.
verses.Psalms 45:11: So shall the king greatly desire thy beauty: for he is thy Lord; and worship thou him.
verses.Psalms 45:14: She shall be brought unto the king in raiment of needlework: the virgins her companions that follow her shall be brought unto thee.
verses.Psalms 45:15: With gladness and rejoicing shall they be brought: they shall enter into the king’s palace.
verses.Psalms 45:17: I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: therefore shall the people praise thee for ever and ever.
verses.Psalms 46:2: Therefore will not we fear, though the earth be removed, and though the mountains be carried into the midst of the sea;
verses.Psalms 46:10: Be still, and know that I am God: I will be exalted among the heathen, I will be exalted in the earth.
verses.Psalms 47:6: Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises unto our King, sing praises.
verses.Psalms 47:7: For God is the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.
verses.Psalms 48:1: Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised in the city of our God, in the mountain of his holiness.
verses.Psalms 48:10: According to thy name, O God, so is thy praise unto the ends of the earth: thy right hand is full of righteousness.
verses.Psalms 48:14: For this God is our God for ever and ever: he will be our guide even unto death.
verses.Psalms 49:1: Hear this, all ye people; give ear, all ye inhabitants of the world:
verses.Psalms 49:9: That he should still live for ever, and not see corruption.
verses.Psalms 49:12: Nevertheless man being in honour abideth not: he is like the beasts that perish.
verses.Psalms 49:16: Be not thou afraid when one is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased;
verses.Psalms 49:19: He shall go to the generation of his fathers; they shall never see light.
verses.Psalms 50:3: Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about him.
verses.Psalms 50:11: I know all the fowls of the mountains: and the wild beasts of the field are mine.
verses.Psalms 50:12: If I were hungry, I would not tell thee: for the world is mine, and the fulness thereof.
verses.Psalms 50:13: Will I eat the flesh of bulls, or drink the blood of goats?
verses.Psalms 50:14: Offer unto God thanksgiving; and pay thy vows unto the most High:
verses.Psalms 50:15: And call upon me in the day of trouble: I will deliver thee, and thou shalt glorify me.
verses.Psalms 50:19: Thou givest thy mouth to evil, and thy tongue frameth deceit.
verses.Psalms 51:2: Wash me throughly from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
verses.Psalms 51:3: For I acknowledge my transgressions: and my sin is ever before me.
verses.Psalms 51:5: Behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me.
verses.Psalms 51:10: Create in me a clean heart, O God; and renew a right spirit within me.
verses.Psalms 51:11: Cast me not away from thy presence; and take not thy holy spirit from me.
verses.Psalms 51:17: The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: a broken and a contrite heart, O God, thou wilt not despise.

verses.Psalms 55:2: Attend unto me, and hear me: I mourn in my complaint, and make a noise;
verses.Psalms 55:3: Because of the voice of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked: for they cast iniquity upon me, and in wrath they hate me.
verses.Psalms 55:7: Lo, then would I wander far off, and remain in the wilderness. Selah.
verses.Psalms 55:11: Wickedness is in the midst thereof: deceit and guile depart not from her streets.
verses.Psalms 55:13: But it was thou, a man mine equal, my guide, and mine acquaintance.
verses.Psalms 55:16: As for me, I will call upon God; and the LORD shall save me.
verses.Psalms 55:18: He hath delivered my soul in peace from the battle that was against me: for there were many with me.
verses.Psalms 55:19: God shall hear, and afflict them, even he that abideth of old. Selah. Because they have no changes, therefore they fear not God.
verses.Psalms 56:4: In God I will praise his word, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
verses.Psalms 56:5: Every day they wrest my words: all their thoughts are against me for evil.
verses.Psalms 56:7: Shall they escape by iniquity? in thine anger cast down the people, O God.
verses.Psalms 56:9: When I cry unto thee, then shall mine enemies turn back: this I know; for God is for me.
verses.Psalms 56:10: In God will I praise his word: in the LORD will I praise his word.
verses.Psalms 56:11: In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
verses.Psalms 56:12: Thy vows are upon me, O God: I will render praises unto thee.
verses.Psalms 56:13: For thou hast delivered my soul from death: wilt not thou deliver my feet from falling, that I may walk before God in the light of the living?
verses.Psalms 57:2: I will cry unto God most high; unto God that performeth all things for me.
verses.Psalms 57:3: He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.
verses.Psalms 58:6: Break their teeth, O God, in their mouth: break out the great teeth of the young lions, O LORD.
verses.Psalms 58:8: As a snail which melteth, let every one of them pass away: like the untimely birth of a woman, that they may not see the sun.
verses.Psalms 59:1: Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.
verses.Psalms 59:10: The God of my mercy shall prevent me: God shall let me see my desire upon mine enemies.
verses.Psalms 60:4: Thou hast given a banner to them that fear thee, that it may be displayed because of the truth. Selah.
verses.Psalms 60:9: Who will bring me into the strong city? who will lead me into Edom?
verses.Psalms 61:1: Hear my cry, O God; attend unto my prayer.
verses.Psalms 62:5: My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him.
verses.Psalms 62:12: Also unto thee, O Lord, belongeth mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.
verses.Psalms 63:4: Thus will I bless thee while I live: I will lift up my hands in thy name.
verses.Psalms 63:9: But those that seek my soul, to destroy it, shall go into the lower parts of the earth.
verses.Psalms 63:10: They shall fall by the sword: they shall be a portion for foxes.
verses.Psalms 64:2: Hide me from the secret counsel of the wicked; from the insurrection of the workers of iniquity:
verses.Psalms 64:8: So they shall make their own tongue to fall upon themselves: all that see them shall flee away.
verses.Psalms 64:9: And all men shall fear, and shall declare the work of God; for they shall wisely consider of his doing.
verses.Psalms 65:2: O thou that hearest prayer, unto thee shall all flesh come.
verses.Psalms 65:3: Iniquities prevail against me: as for our transgressions, thou shalt purge them away.
verses.Psalms 65:4: Blessed is the man whom thou choosest, and causest to approach unto thee, that he may dwell in thy courts: we shall be satisfied with the goodness …
verses.Psalms 65:12: They drop upon the pastures of the wilderness: and the little hills rejoice on every side.
verses.Psalms 66:1: Make a joyful noise unto God, all ye lands:
verses.Psalms 66:18: If I regard iniquity in my heart, the Lord will not hear me:
verses.Psalms 66:19: But verily God hath heard me; he hath attended to the voice of my prayer.
verses.Psalms 67:1: God be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine upon us; Selah.
verses.Psalms 67:2: That thy way may be known upon earth, thy saving health among all nations.
verses.Psalms 67:3: Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.
verses.Psalms 67:5: Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.
verses.Psalms 68:4: Sing unto God, sing praises to his name: extol him that rideth upon the heavens by his name JAH, and rejoice before him.
verses.Psalms 68:19: Blessed be the Lord, who daily loadeth us with benefits, even the God of our salvation. Selah.

verses.Psalms 68:27: There is little Benjamin with their ruler, the princes of Judah and their council, the princes of Zebulun, and the princes of Naphtali.
verses.Psalms 68:32: Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises unto the Lord; Selah:
verses.Psalms 69:8: I am become a stranger unto my brethren, and an alien unto my mother’s children.
verses.Psalms 69:10: When I wept, and chastened my soul with fasting, that was to my reproach.
verses.Psalms 69:11: I made sackcloth also my garment; and I became a proverb to them.
verses.Psalms 69:12: They that sit in the gate speak against me; and I was the song of the drunkards.
verses.Psalms 69:17: And hide not thy face from thy servant; for I am in trouble: hear me speedily.
verses.Psalms 69:18: Draw nigh unto my soul, and redeem it: deliver me because of mine enemies.
verses.Psalms 69:21: They gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
verses.Psalms 69:36: The seed also of his servants shall inherit it: and they that love his name shall dwell therein.
verses.Psalms 70:1: Make haste, O God, to deliver me; make haste to help me, O Lord.
verses.Psalms 70:4: Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee: and let such as love thy salvation say continually, Let God be magnified.
verses.Psalms 70:5: But I am poor and needy: make haste unto me, O God: thou art my help and my deliverer; O LORD, make no tarrying.
verses.Psalms 71:4: Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
verses.Psalms 71:5: For thou art my hope, O Lord GOD: thou art my trust from my youth.
verses.Psalms 71:7: I am as a wonder unto many; but thou art my strong refuge.
verses.Psalms 71:9: Cast me not off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength faileth.
verses.Psalms 71:10: For mine enemies speak against me; and they that lay wait for my soul take counsel together,
verses.Psalms 71:12: O God, be not far from me: O my God, make haste for my help.
verses.Psalms 71:14: But I will hope continually, and will yet praise thee more and more.
verses.Psalms 71:15: My mouth shall shew forth thy righteousness and thy salvation all the day; for I know not the numbers thereof.
verses.Psalms 71:19: Thy righteousness also, O God, is very high, who hast done great things: O God, who is like unto thee!
verses.Psalms 71:22: I will also praise thee with the psaltery, even thy truth, O my God: unto thee will I sing with the harp, O thou Holy One of Israel.
verses.Psalms 71:23: My lips shall greatly rejoice when I sing unto thee; and my soul, which thou hast redeemed.
verses.Psalms 72:2: He shall judge thy people with righteousness, and thy poor with judgment.
verses.Psalms 72:3: The mountains shall bring peace to the people, and the little hills, by righteousness.
verses.Psalms 72:4: He shall judge the poor of the people, he shall save the children of the needy, and shall break in pieces the oppressor.
verses.Psalms 72:8: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river unto the ends of the earth.
verses.Psalms 72:12: For he shall deliver the needy when he crieth; the poor also, and him that hath no helper.
verses.Psalms 72:13: He shall spare the poor and needy, and shall save the souls of the needy.
verses.Psalms 72:14: He shall redeem their soul from deceit and violence: and precious shall their blood be in his sight.
verses.Psalms 73:1: Truly God is good to Israel, even to such as are of a clean heart.

verses.Psalms 73:16: When I thought to know this, it was too painful for me;
verses.Psalms 73:27: For, lo, they that are far from thee shall perish: thou hast destroyed all them that go a whoring from thee.
verses.Psalms 74:3: Lift up thy feet unto the perpetual desolations; even all that the enemy hath done wickedly in the sanctuary.

verses.Psalms 74:12: For God is my King of old, working salvation in the midst of the earth.
verses.Psalms 74:15: Thou didst cleave the fountain and the flood: thou driedst up mighty rivers.
verses.Psalms 74:19: O deliver not the soul of thy turtledove unto the multitude of the wicked: forget not the congregation of thy poor for ever.
verses.Psalms 74:22: Arise, O God, plead thine own cause: remember how the foolish man reproacheth thee daily.
verses.Psalms 75:3: The earth and all the inhabitants thereof are dissolved: I bear up the pillars of it. Selah.
verses.Psalms 75:9: But I will declare for ever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
verses.Psalms 76:7: Thou, even thou, art to be feared: and who may stand in thy sight when once thou art angry?
verses.Psalms 76:9: When God arose to judgment, to save all the meek of the earth. Selah.
verses.Psalms 77:5: I have considered the days of old, the years of ancient times.
verses.Psalms 77:6: I call to remembrance my song in the night: I commune with mine own heart: and my spirit made diligent search.

verses.Psalms 77:8: Is his mercy clean gone for ever? doth his promise fail for evermore?
verses.Psalms 77:10: And I said, This is my infirmity: but I will remember the years of the right hand of the most High.
verses.Psalms 77:13: Thy way, O God, is in the sanctuary: who is so great a God as our God?
verses.Psalms 77:19: Thy way is in the sea, and thy path in the great waters, and thy footsteps are not known.
verses.Psalms 78:1: Give ear, O my people, to my law: incline your ears to the words of my mouth.

verses.Psalms 78:22: Because they believed not in God, and trusted not in his salvation:

verses.Psalms 79:9: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of thy name: and deliver us, and purge away our sins, for thy name’s sake.

verses.Psalms 80:3: Turn us again, O God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

verses.Psalms 80:18: So will not we go back from thee: quicken us, and we will call upon thy name.
verses.Psalms 80:19: Turn us again, O LORD God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.
verses.Psalms 81:1: Sing aloud unto God our strength: make a joyful noise unto the God of Jacob.

verses.Psalms 81:4: For this was a statute for Israel, and a law of the God of Jacob.

verses.Psalms 82:3: Defend the poor and fatherless: do justice to the afflicted and needy.
verses.Psalms 82:4: Deliver the poor and needy: rid them out of the hand of the wicked.
verses.Psalms 83:1: Keep not thou silence, O God: hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God.
verses.Psalms 83:3: They have taken crafty counsel against thy people, and consulted against thy hidden ones.
verses.Psalms 83:5: For they have consulted together with one consent: they are confederate against thee:
verses.Psalms 83:12: Who said, Let us take to ourselves the houses of God in possession.
verses.Psalms 83:14: As the fire burneth a wood, and as the flame setteth the mountains on fire;
verses.Psalms 83:18: That men may know that thou, whose name alone is JEHOVAH, art the most high over all the earth.
verses.Psalms 84:9: Behold, O God our shield, and look upon the face of thine anointed.
verses.Psalms 85:1: Lord, thou hast been favourable unto thy land: thou hast brought back the captivity of Jacob.
verses.Psalms 85:3: Thou hast taken away all thy wrath: thou hast turned thyself from the fierceness of thine anger.
verses.Psalms 85:4: Turn us, O God of our salvation, and cause thine anger toward us to cease.
verses.Psalms 85:5: Wilt thou be angry with us for ever? wilt thou draw out thine anger to all generations?
verses.Psalms 85:7: Shew us thy mercy, O LORD, and grant us thy salvation.
verses.Psalms 85:10: Mercy and truth are met together; righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
verses.Psalms 85:13: Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
verses.Psalms 86:4: Rejoice the soul of thy servant: for unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
verses.Psalms 86:6: Give ear, O LORD, unto my prayer; and attend to the voice of my supplications.
verses.Psalms 86:8: Among the gods there is none like unto thee, O Lord; neither are there any works like unto thy works.
verses.Psalms 86:10: For thou art great, and doest wondrous things: thou art God alone.
verses.Psalms 86:13: For great is thy mercy toward me: and thou hast delivered my soul from the lowest hell.
verses.Psalms 86:14: O God, the proud are risen against me, and the assemblies of violent men have sought after my soul; and have not set thee before them.
verses.Psalms 87:6: The LORD shall count, when he writeth up the people, that this man was born there. Selah.
verses.Psalms 88:1: O lord God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee:
verses.Psalms 88:3: For my soul is full of troubles: and my life draweth nigh unto the grave.
verses.Psalms 88:12: Shall thy wonders be known in the dark? and thy righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?
verses.Psalms 88:14: LORD, why castest thou off my soul? why hidest thou thy face from me?
verses.Psalms 88:18: Lover and friend hast thou put far from me, and mine acquaintance into darkness.
verses.Psalms 89:4: Thy seed will I establish for ever, and build up thy throne to all generations. Selah.
verses.Psalms 89:14: Justice and judgment are the habitation of thy throne: mercy and truth shall go before thy face.

verses.Psalms 89:33: Nevertheless my lovingkindness will I not utterly take from him, nor suffer my faithfulness to fail.
verses.Psalms 89:35: Once have I sworn by my holiness that I will not lie unto David.
verses.Psalms 89:37: It shall be established for ever as the moon, and as a faithful witness in heaven. Selah.

verses.Psalms 89:43: Thou hast also turned the edge of his sword, and hast not made him to stand in the battle.
verses.Psalms 89:46: How long, LORD? wilt thou hide thyself for ever? shall thy wrath burn like fire?
verses.Psalms 89:47: Remember how short my time is: wherefore hast thou made all men in vain?
verses.Psalms 90:7: For we are consumed by thine anger, and by thy wrath are we troubled.
verses.Psalms 90:8: Thou hast set our iniquities before thee, our secret sins in the light of thy countenance.

verses.Psalms 92:4: For thou, LORD, hast made me glad through thy work: I will triumph in the works of thy hands.

verses.Psalms 94:19: In the multitude of my thoughts within me thy comforts delight my soul.
verses.Psalms 95:1: O come, let us sing unto the LORD: let us make a joyful noise to the rock of our salvation.

verses.Psalms 96:6: Honour and majesty are before him: strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.
verses.Psalms 96:11: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof.

verses.Psalms 96:13: Before the LORD: for he cometh, for he cometh to judge the earth: he shall judge the world with righteousness, and the people with his truth.

verses.Psalms 101:2: I will behave myself wisely in a perfect way. O when wilt thou come unto me? I will walk within my house with a perfect heart.

verses.Psalms 102:10: Because of thine indignation and thy wrath: for thou hast lifted me up, and cast me down.

verses.Psalms 102:27: But thou art the same, and thy years shall have no end.
verses.Psalms 103:2: Bless the LORD, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits:

verses.Psalms 103:8: The LORD is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and plenteous in mercy.
verses.Psalms 103:16: For the wind passeth over it, and it is gone; and the place thereof shall know it no more.
verses.Psalms 103:18: To such as keep his covenant, and to those that remember his commandments to do them.

verses.Psalms 106:1: Praise ye the LORD. O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 106:2: Who can utter the mighty acts of the LORD? who can shew forth all his praise?

verses.Psalms 106:20: Thus they changed their glory into the similitude of an ox that eateth grass.

verses.Psalms 106:33: Because they provoked his spirit, so that he spake unadvisedly with his lips.
verses.Psalms 106:36: And they served their idols: which were a snare unto them.

verses.Psalms 107:1: O give thanks unto the LORD, for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 107:5: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted in them.
verses.Psalms 107:9: For he satisfieth the longing soul, and filleth the hungry soul with goodness.

verses.Psalms 107:24: These see the works of the LORD, and his wonders in the deep.
verses.Psalms 107:28: Then they cry unto the LORD in their trouble, and he bringeth them out of their distresses.
verses.Psalms 107:37: And sow the fields, and plant vineyards, which may yield fruits of increase.

verses.Psalms 108:3: I will praise thee, O LORD, among the people: and I will sing praises unto thee among the nations.

verses.Psalms 108:11: Wilt not thou, O God, who hast cast us off? and wilt not thou, O God, go forth with our hosts?
verses.Psalms 109:1: Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise;
verses.Psalms 109:4: For my love they are my adversaries: but I give myself unto prayer.

verses.Psalms 109:24: My knees are weak through fasting; and my flesh faileth of fatness.

verses.Psalms 109:26: Help me, O LORD my God: O save me according to thy mercy:
verses.Psalms 109:27: That they may know that this is thy hand; that thou, LORD, hast done it.
verses.Psalms 110:2: The LORD shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.

verses.Psalms 111:2: The works of the LORD are great, sought out of all them that have pleasure therein.
verses.Psalms 111:4: He hath made his wonderful works to be remembered: the LORD is gracious and full of compassion.
verses.Psalms 111:6: He hath shewed his people the power of his works, that he may give them the heritage of the heathen.
verses.Psalms 112:4: Unto the upright there ariseth light in the darkness: he is gracious, and full of compassion, and righteous.
verses.Psalms 112:5: A good man sheweth favour, and lendeth: he will guide his affairs with discretion.
verses.Psalms 112:6: Surely he shall not be moved for ever: the righteous shall be in everlasting remembrance.
verses.Psalms 113:8: That he may set him with princes, even with the princes of his people.
verses.Psalms 114:1: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of strange language;
verses.Psalms 114:4: The mountains skipped like rams, and the little hills like lambs.
verses.Psalms 114:5: What ailed thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou Jordan, that thou wast driven back?
verses.Psalms 114:6: Ye mountains, that ye skipped like rams; and ye little hills, like lambs?
verses.Psalms 114:7: Tremble, thou earth, at the presence of the Lord, at the presence of the God of Jacob;
verses.Psalms 115:1: Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory, for thy mercy, and for thy truth’s sake.
verses.Psalms 115:3: But our God is in the heavens: he hath done whatsoever he hath pleased.
verses.Psalms 115:10: O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD: he is their help and their shield.
verses.Psalms 115:13: He will bless them that fear the LORD, both small and great.
verses.Psalms 115:16: The heaven, even the heavens, are the LORD’s: but the earth hath he given to the children of men.
verses.Psalms 116:5: Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; yea, our God is merciful.
verses.Psalms 116:6: The LORD preserveth the simple: I was brought low, and he helped me.
verses.Psalms 116:8: For thou hast delivered my soul from death, mine eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.
verses.Psalms 116:12: What shall I render unto the LORD for all his benefits toward me?
verses.Psalms 116:16: O LORD, truly I am thy servant; I am thy servant, and the son of thine handmaid: thou hast loosed my bonds.
verses.Psalms 117:1: O praise the LORD, all ye nations: praise him, all ye people.
verses.Psalms 117:2: For his merciful kindness is great toward us: and the truth of the LORD endureth for ever. Praise ye the LORD.
verses.Psalms 118:1: O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: because his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 118:6: The LORD is on my side; I will not fear: what can man do unto me?
verses.Psalms 118:9: It is better to trust in the LORD than to put confidence in princes.
verses.Psalms 118:10: All nations compassed me about: but in the name of the LORD will I destroy them.
verses.Psalms 118:14: The LORD is my strength and song, and is become my salvation.
verses.Psalms 118:17: I shall not die, but live, and declare the works of the LORD.
verses.Psalms 118:18: The LORD hath chastened me sore: but he hath not given me over unto death.
verses.Psalms 118:20: This gate of the LORD, into which the righteous shall enter.
verses.Psalms 118:21: I will praise thee: for thou hast heard me, and art become my salvation.
verses.Psalms 118:22: The stone which the builders refused is become the head stone of the corner.
verses.Psalms 118:23: This is the LORD’s doing; it is marvellous in our eyes.
verses.Psalms 118:24: This is the day which the LORD hath made; we will rejoice and be glad in it.
verses.Psalms 118:27: God is the LORD, which hath shewed us light: bind the sacrifice with cords, even unto the horns of the altar.
verses.Psalms 118:29: O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 119:5: O that my ways were directed to keep thy statutes!
verses.Psalms 119:6: Then shall I not be ashamed, when I have respect unto all thy commandments.
verses.Psalms 119:8: I will keep thy statutes: O forsake me not utterly.
verses.Psalms 119:13: With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.
verses.Psalms 119:14: I have rejoiced in the way of thy testimonies, as much as in all riches.
verses.Psalms 119:16: I will delight myself in thy statutes: I will not forget thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:19: I am a stranger in the earth: hide not thy commandments from me.
verses.Psalms 119:21: Thou hast rebuked the proud that are cursed, which do err from thy commandments.
verses.Psalms 119:22: Remove from me reproach and contempt; for I have kept thy testimonies.
verses.Psalms 119:24: Thy testimonies also are my delight and my counsellors.
verses.Psalms 119:25: My soul cleaveth unto the dust: quicken thou me according to thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:30: I have chosen the way of truth: thy judgments have I laid before me.
verses.Psalms 119:31: I have stuck unto thy testimonies: O LORD, put me not to shame.
verses.Psalms 119:33: Teach me, O LORD, the way of thy statutes; and I shall keep it unto the end.
verses.Psalms 119:35: Make me to go in the path of thy commandments; for therein do I delight.
verses.Psalms 119:37: Turn away mine eyes from beholding vanity; and quicken thou me in thy way.
verses.Psalms 119:41: Let thy mercies come also unto me, O LORD, even thy salvation, according to thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:44: So shall I keep thy law continually for ever and ever.
verses.Psalms 119:46: I will speak of thy testimonies also before kings, and will not be ashamed.
verses.Psalms 119:47: And I will delight myself in thy commandments, which I have loved.
verses.Psalms 119:54: Thy statutes have been my songs in the house of my pilgrimage.
verses.Psalms 119:57: Thou art my portion, O LORD: I have said that I would keep thy words.
verses.Psalms 119:58: I intreated thy favour with my whole heart: be merciful unto me according to thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:60: I made haste, and delayed not to keep thy commandments.
verses.Psalms 119:62: At midnight I will rise to give thanks unto thee because of thy righteous judgments.
verses.Psalms 119:64: The earth, O LORD, is full of thy mercy: teach me thy statutes.
verses.Psalms 119:66: Teach me good judgment and knowledge: for I have believed thy commandments.
verses.Psalms 119:71: It is good for me that I have been afflicted; that I might learn thy statutes.
verses.Psalms 119:76: Let, I pray thee, thy merciful kindness be for my comfort, according to thy word unto thy servant.
verses.Psalms 119:80: Let my heart be sound in thy statutes; that I be not ashamed.
verses.Psalms 119:81: My soul fainteth for thy salvation: but I hope in thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:83: For I am become like a bottle in the smoke; yet do I not forget thy statutes.
verses.Psalms 119:85: The proud have digged pits for me, which are not after thy law.
verses.Psalms 119:86: All thy commandments are faithful: they persecute me wrongfully; help thou me.
verses.Psalms 119:87: They had almost consumed me upon earth; but I forsook not thy precepts.
verses.Psalms 119:89: For ever, O LORD, thy word is settled in heaven.
verses.Psalms 119:95: The wicked have waited for me to destroy me: but I will consider thy testimonies.
verses.Psalms 119:97: O how I love thy law! it is my meditation all the day.
verses.Psalms 119:98: Thou through thy commandments hast made me wiser than mine enemies: for they are ever with me.
verses.Psalms 119:99: I have more understanding than all my teachers: for thy testimonies are my meditation.
verses.Psalms 119:100: I understand more than the ancients, because I keep thy precepts.
verses.Psalms 119:101: I have refrained my feet from every evil way, that I might keep thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:105: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
verses.Psalms 119:111: Thy testimonies have I taken as an heritage for ever: for they are the rejoicing of my heart.
verses.Psalms 119:114: Thou art my hiding place and my shield: I hope in thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:118: Thou hast trodden down all them that err from thy statutes: for their deceit is falsehood.
verses.Psalms 119:121: I have done judgment and justice: leave me not to mine oppressors.
verses.Psalms 119:125: I am thy servant; give me understanding, that I may know thy testimonies.
verses.Psalms 119:129: Thy testimonies are wonderful: therefore doth my soul keep them.
verses.Psalms 119:135: Make thy face to shine upon thy servant; and teach me thy statutes.
verses.Psalms 119:137: Righteous art thou, O LORD, and upright are thy judgments.
verses.Psalms 119:138: Thy testimonies that thou hast commanded are righteous and very faithful.
verses.Psalms 119:140: Thy word is very pure: therefore thy servant loveth it.
verses.Psalms 119:141: I am small and despised: yet do not I forget thy precepts.
verses.Psalms 119:142: Thy righteousness is an everlasting righteousness, and thy law is the truth.
verses.Psalms 119:145: I cried with my whole heart; hear me, O LORD: I will keep thy statutes.
verses.Psalms 119:150: They draw nigh that follow after mischief: they are far from thy law.
verses.Psalms 119:151: Thou art near, O LORD; and all thy commandments are truth.
verses.Psalms 119:154: Plead my cause, and deliver me: quicken me according to thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:157: Many are my persecutors and mine enemies; yet do I not decline from thy testimonies.
verses.Psalms 119:166: LORD, I have hoped for thy salvation, and done thy commandments.
verses.Psalms 119:169: Let my cry come near before thee, O LORD: give me understanding according to thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:170: Let my supplication come before thee: deliver me according to thy word.
verses.Psalms 119:171: My lips shall utter praise, when thou hast taught me thy statutes.
verses.Psalms 121:6: The sun shall not smite thee by day, nor the moon by night.
verses.Psalms 121:8: The LORD shall preserve thy going out and thy coming in from this time forth, and even for evermore.
verses.Psalms 122:2: Our feet shall stand within thy gates, O Jerusalem.
verses.Psalms 123:2: Behold, as the eyes of servants look unto the hand of their masters, and as the eyes of a maiden unto the hand of her mistress; so our eyes wait up…
verses.Psalms 124:1: If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, now may Israel say;
verses.Psalms 124:6: Blessed be the LORD, who hath not given us as a prey to their teeth.
verses.Psalms 124:7: Our soul is escaped as a bird out of the snare of the fowlers: the snare is broken, and we are escaped.
verses.Psalms 124:8: Our help is in the name of the LORD, who made heaven and earth.
verses.Psalms 125:1: They that trust in the LORD shall be as mount Zion, which cannot be removed, but abideth for ever.
verses.Psalms 126:5: They that sow in tears shall reap in joy.
verses.Psalms 127:3: Lo, children are an heritage of the LORD: and the fruit of the womb is his reward.
verses.Psalms 127:5: Happy is the man that hath his quiver full of them: they shall not be ashamed, but they shall speak with the enemies in the gate.
verses.Psalms 129:7: Wherewith the mower filleth not his hand; nor he that bindeth sheaves his bosom.
verses.Psalms 130:1: Out of the depths have I cried unto thee, O LORD.
verses.Psalms 130:3: If thou, LORD, shouldest mark iniquities, O Lord, who shall stand?
verses.Psalms 130:4: But there is forgiveness with thee, that thou mayest be feared.
verses.Psalms 130:6: My soul waiteth for the Lord more than they that watch for the morning: I say, more than they that watch for the morning.
verses.Psalms 131:1: Lord, my heart is not haughty, nor mine eyes lofty: neither do I exercise myself in great matters, or in things too high for me.
verses.Psalms 132:1: Lord, remember David, and all his afflictions:
verses.Psalms 132:4: I will not give sleep to mine eyes, or slumber to mine eyelids,
verses.Psalms 132:11: The LORD hath sworn in truth unto David; he will not turn from it; Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy throne.
verses.Psalms 132:12: If thy children will keep my covenant and my testimony that I shall teach them, their children shall also sit upon thy throne for evermore.
verses.Psalms 132:18: His enemies will I clothe with shame: but upon himself shall his crown flourish.
verses.Psalms 133:3: As the dew of Hermon, and as the dew that descended upon the mountains of Zion: for there the LORD commanded the blessing, even life for evermore.
verses.Psalms 134:1: Behold, bless ye the LORD, all ye servants of the LORD, which by night stand in the house of the LORD.
verses.Psalms 135:1: Praise ye the LORD. Praise ye the name of the LORD; praise him, O ye servants of the LORD.
verses.Psalms 135:2: Ye that stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God.
verses.Psalms 135:3: Praise the LORD; for the LORD is good: sing praises unto his name; for it is pleasant.
verses.Psalms 135:7: He causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings for the rain; he bringeth the wind out of his treasuries.
verses.Psalms 135:15: The idols of the heathen are silver and gold, the work of men’s hands.
verses.Psalms 135:16: They have mouths, but they speak not; eyes have they, but they see not;
verses.Psalms 135:20: Bless the LORD, O house of Levi: ye that fear the LORD, bless the LORD.
verses.Psalms 136:1: O give thanks unto the LORD; for he is good: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 136:2: O give thanks unto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 136:5: To him that by wisdom made the heavens: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 136:8: The sun to rule by day: for his mercy endureth for ever:
verses.Psalms 136:9: The moon and stars to rule by night: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 136:11: And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for ever:
verses.Psalms 136:13: To him which divided the Red sea into parts: for his mercy endureth for ever:
verses.Psalms 136:15: But overthrew Pharaoh and his host in the Red sea: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 136:26: O give thanks unto the God of heaven: for his mercy endureth for ever.
verses.Psalms 137:1: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yea, we wept, when we remembered Zion.
verses.Psalms 137:2: We hanged our harps upon the willows in the midst thereof.
verses.Psalms 138:1: I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise unto thee.
verses.Psalms 139:4: For there is not a word in my tongue, but, lo, O LORD, thou knowest it altogether.
verses.Psalms 139:5: Thou hast beset me behind and before, and laid thine hand upon me.
verses.Psalms 139:6: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I cannot attain unto it.
verses.Psalms 139:7: Whither shall I go from thy spirit? or whither shall I flee from thy presence?
verses.Psalms 139:8: If I ascend up into heaven, thou art there: if I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there.
verses.Psalms 139:11: If I say, Surely the darkness shall cover me; even the night shall be light about me.
verses.Psalms 139:17: How precious also are thy thoughts unto me, O God! how great is the sum of them!
verses.Psalms 139:20: For they speak against thee wickedly, and thine enemies take thy name in vain.
verses.Psalms 139:22: I hate them with perfect hatred: I count them mine enemies.
verses.Psalms 139:23: Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts:
verses.Psalms 140:1: Deliver me, O LORD, from the evil man: preserve me from the violent man;
verses.Psalms 140:13: Surely the righteous shall give thanks unto thy name: the upright shall dwell in thy presence.
verses.Psalms 141:1: Lord, I cry unto thee: make haste unto me; give ear unto my voice, when I cry unto thee.
verses.Psalms 141:4: Incline not my heart to any evil thing, to practise wicked works with men that work iniquity: and let me not eat of their dainties.
verses.Psalms 142:2: I poured out my complaint before him; I shewed before him my trouble.
verses.Psalms 142:5: I cried unto thee, O LORD: I said, Thou art my refuge and my portion in the land of the living.
verses.Psalms 142:6: Attend unto my cry; for I am brought very low: deliver me from my persecutors; for they are stronger than I.
verses.Psalms 143:2: And enter not into judgment with thy servant: for in thy sight shall no man living be justified.
verses.Psalms 143:6: I stretch forth my hands unto thee: my soul thirsteth after thee, as a thirsty land. Selah.
verses.Psalms 143:8: Cause me to hear thy lovingkindness in the morning; for in thee do I trust: cause me to know the way wherein I should walk; for I lift up my soul u…
verses.Psalms 143:11: Quicken me, O LORD, for thy name’s sake: for thy righteousness’ sake bring my soul out of trouble.
verses.Psalms 143:12: And of thy mercy cut off mine enemies, and destroy all them that afflict my soul: for I am thy servant.
verses.Psalms 144:1: Blessed be the LORD my strength, which teacheth my hands to war, and my fingers to fight:
verses.Psalms 144:3: LORD, what is man, that thou takest knowledge of him! or the son of man, that thou makest account of him!
verses.Psalms 144:4: Man is like to vanity: his days are as a shadow that passeth away.
verses.Psalms 145:1: I will extol thee, my God, O king; and I will bless thy name for ever and ever.
verses.Psalms 145:8: The LORD is gracious, and full of compassion; slow to anger, and of great mercy.
verses.Psalms 145:9: The LORD is good to all: and his tender mercies are over all his works.
verses.Psalms 145:13: Thy kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and thy dominion endureth throughout all generations.
verses.Psalms 145:16: Thou openest thine hand, and satisfiest the desire of every living thing.
verses.Psalms 146:2: While I live will I praise the LORD: I will sing praises unto my God while I have any being.
verses.Psalms 146:6: Which made heaven, and earth, the sea, and all that therein is: which keepeth truth for ever:
verses.Psalms 146:9: The LORD preserveth the strangers; he relieveth the fatherless and widow: but the way of the wicked he turneth upside down.
verses.Psalms 146:10: The LORD shall reign for ever, even thy God, O Zion, unto all generations. Praise ye the LORD.
verses.Psalms 147:1: Praise ye the LORD: for it is good to sing praises unto our God; for it is pleasant; and praise is comely.
verses.Psalms 147:8: Who covereth the heaven with clouds, who prepareth rain for the earth, who maketh grass to grow upon the mountains.
verses.Psalms 147:9: He giveth to the beast his food, and to the young ravens which cry.
verses.Psalms 147:16: He giveth snow like wool: he scattereth the hoarfrost like ashes.
verses.Psalms 148:1: Praise ye the LORD. Praise ye the LORD from the heavens: praise him in the heights.
verses.Psalms 148:5: Let them praise the name of the LORD: for he commanded, and they were created.
verses.Psalms 148:9: Mountains, and all hills; fruitful trees, and all cedars:
verses.Psalms 149:6: Let the high praises of God be in their mouth, and a two-edged sword in their hand;
verses.Psalms 149:8: To bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with fetters of iron;
verses.Psalms 150:5: Praise him upon the loud cymbals: praise him upon the high sounding cymbals.
verses.Proverbs 1:1: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;
verses.Proverbs 1:3: To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;
verses.Proverbs 1:8: My son, hear the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
verses.Proverbs 1:12: Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit:
verses.Proverbs 1:16: For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood.
verses.Proverbs 1:18: And they lay wait for their own blood; they lurk privily for their own lives.
verses.Proverbs 1:20: Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:
verses.Proverbs 1:24: Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded;
verses.Proverbs 1:25: But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:
verses.Proverbs 1:31: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own devices.
verses.Proverbs 1:32: For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of fools shall destroy them.
verses.Proverbs 2:2: So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
verses.Proverbs 2:10: When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul;
verses.Proverbs 2:18: For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.
verses.Proverbs 2:20: That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
verses.Proverbs 2:21: For the upright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remain in it.
verses.Proverbs 2:22: But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the transgressors shall be rooted out of it.
verses.Proverbs 3:5: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
verses.Proverbs 3:9: Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase:
verses.Proverbs 3:26: For the LORD shall be thy confidence, and shall keep thy foot from being taken.
verses.Proverbs 3:27: Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it.
verses.Proverbs 3:28: Say not unto thy neighbour, Go, and come again, and to morrow I will give; when thou hast it by thee.
verses.Proverbs 3:29: Devise not evil against thy neighbour, seeing he dwelleth securely by thee.
verses.Proverbs 4:1: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend to know understanding.
verses.Proverbs 4:13: Take fast hold of instruction; let her not go: keep her; for she is thy life.
verses.Proverbs 4:17: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.
verses.Proverbs 4:25: Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.
verses.Proverbs 4:26: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.
verses.Proverbs 5:1: My son, attend unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my understanding:
verses.Proverbs 5:2: That thou mayest regard discretion, and that thy lips may keep knowledge.
verses.Proverbs 5:8: Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:
verses.Proverbs 5:9: Lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel:
verses.Proverbs 5:13: And have not obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor inclined mine ear to them that instructed me!
verses.Proverbs 5:17: Let them be only thine own, and not strangers’ with thee.
verses.Proverbs 5:18: Let thy fountain be blessed: and rejoice with the wife of thy youth.
verses.Proverbs 5:19: Let her be as the loving hind and pleasant roe; let her breasts satisfy thee at all times; and be thou ravished always with her love.
verses.Proverbs 5:21: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, and he pondereth all his goings.
verses.Proverbs 6:2: Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth, thou art taken with the words of thy mouth.
verses.Proverbs 6:3: Do this now, my son, and deliver thyself, when thou art come into the hand of thy friend; go, humble thyself, and make sure thy friend.
verses.Proverbs 6:4: Give not sleep to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids.
verses.Proverbs 6:7: Which having no guide, overseer, or ruler,
verses.Proverbs 6:8: Provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest.
verses.Proverbs 6:9: How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?
verses.Proverbs 6:15: Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; suddenly shall he be broken without remedy.
verses.Proverbs 6:19: A false witness that speaketh lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren.
verses.Proverbs 6:21: Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck.
verses.Proverbs 6:23: For the commandment is a lamp; and the law is light; and reproofs of instruction are the way of life:
verses.Proverbs 6:25: Lust not after her beauty in thine heart; neither let her take thee with her eyelids.
verses.Proverbs 6:28: Can one go upon hot coals, and his feet not be burned?
verses.Proverbs 6:30: Men do not despise a thief, if he steal to satisfy his soul when he is hungry;
verses.Proverbs 6:32: But whoso committeth adultery with a woman lacketh understanding: he that doeth it destroyeth his own soul.
verses.Proverbs 6:33: A wound and dishonour shall he get; and his reproach shall not be wiped away.
verses.Proverbs 7:13: So she caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him,
verses.Proverbs 7:14: I have peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows.
verses.Proverbs 7:18: Come, let us take our fill of love until the morning: let us solace ourselves with loves.
verses.Proverbs 8:2: She standeth in the top of high places, by the way in the places of the paths.
verses.Proverbs 8:5: O ye simple, understand wisdom: and, ye fools, be ye of an understanding heart.
verses.Proverbs 8:9: They are all plain to him that understandeth, and right to them that find knowledge.
verses.Proverbs 8:10: Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold.
verses.Proverbs 8:17: I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me.
verses.Proverbs 8:18: Riches and honour are with me; yea, durable riches and righteousness.
verses.Proverbs 8:23: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, or ever the earth was.
verses.Proverbs 8:24: When there were no depths, I was brought forth; when there were no fountains abounding with water.
verses.Proverbs 8:25: Before the mountains were settled, before the hills was I brought forth:
verses.Proverbs 8:33: Hear instruction, and be wise, and refuse it not.
verses.Proverbs 9:2: She hath killed her beasts; she hath mingled her wine; she hath also furnished her table.
verses.Proverbs 9:4: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,
verses.Proverbs 9:14: For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,
verses.Proverbs 9:16: Whoso is simple, let him turn in hither: and as for him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,
verses.Proverbs 10:1: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.
verses.Proverbs 10:2: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death.
verses.Proverbs 10:3: The LORD will not suffer the soul of the righteous to famish: but he casteth away the substance of the wicked.
verses.Proverbs 10:6: Blessings are upon the head of the just: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.
verses.Proverbs 10:7: The memory of the just is blessed: but the name of the wicked shall rot.
verses.Proverbs 10:11: The mouth of a righteous man is a well of life: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.
verses.Proverbs 10:14: Wise men lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
verses.Proverbs 10:16: The labour of the righteous tendeth to life: the fruit of the wicked to sin.
verses.Proverbs 10:17: He is in the way of life that keepeth instruction: but he that refuseth reproof erreth.
verses.Proverbs 10:22: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it.
verses.Proverbs 10:23: It is as sport to a fool to do mischief: but a man of understanding hath wisdom.
verses.Proverbs 10:24: The fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him: but the desire of the righteous shall be granted.
verses.Proverbs 11:4: Riches profit not in the day of wrath: but righteousness delivereth from death.
verses.Proverbs 11:23: The desire of the righteous is only good: but the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
verses.Proverbs 11:24: There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth; and there is that withholdeth more than is meet, but it tendeth to poverty.
